description: GOLDEN PROVENANCE registry (docket SB.2, 019f77117376). Records whether each golden was blessed ONCE by an INDEPENDENT authority (an independent Gerber viewer and/or kicad-cli DRC on the round-tripped board). A golden with blessed=false, or with no entry here, is UNTRUSTED: usable by the geometry-diff harness as a DRIFT PIN, but NEVER as a correctness oracle. See tests/oracle/provenance.py and golden/HOW_TO_BLESS.md.

spike-gerber-v1.method: gerbv (homebrew /opt/homebrew/bin/gerbv) visual layer-by-layer overlay of all 8 files vs stated intent — Edge_Cuts, F_Cu, B_Cu, F_Mask, B_Mask, F_SilkS, PTH.drl, NPTH.drl each independently confirmed against the documented geometry (board.yaml + generate.py)
spike-gerber-v1.notes: BLESSED by owner 2026-07-18 via independent gerbv walkthrough (see golden/HOW_TO_BLESS.md). All 8 layers matched intent: 40x30 outline; 4x real 1.2x1.3mm 0805 SMD lands + U1 O1.6 TH pad + O0.8 via + VCC/GND traces on F_Cu; sparse B_Cu; 5 F_Mask openings with via correctly TENTED; single B_Mask opening; 2 courtyards + pin-1 tick on F_SilkS; PTH holes (O0.8 U1, O0.4 via) concentric in their pads; single O3.2 bare NPTH mounting hole. Drill files loaded cleanly in gerbv despite open bug 019f7720928d (G90/G05-after-header) — cosmetic to gerbv, still tracked. This golden is the TRUSTED correctness oracle. KNOWN: the current pcb_worker.gerber emitter DIVERGES from it — placeholder 1.0x0.6mm SMD pads vs these real 1.2x1.3mm 0805 lands (emitter bug 019f7736b236, fixed by FootprintDefinition/IR Stage 2). The correctness-oracle test is xfail(strict) against that bug until Stage 2 lands, at which point it flips to a live passing assertion.

emitter-snapshot-v1.notes: DRIFT-PIN ONLY — captured from the emitter under test (pcb_worker.gerber.build_gerbers via scripts/capture_emitter_golden.py). Inherently circular: it can detect FUTURE drift in the emitter but can NEVER attest correctness. blessed stays false PERMANENTLY by design; do NOT bless this entry. The correctness reference is spike-gerber-v1.
